properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 23 at 1:56PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 156 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 16
miles northeast of Kim, or 32 miles west of Springfield, moving
northeast at 15 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and half inch hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is
possible.

This storm will remain over mainly rural areas of west central Baca
and east central Las Animas Counties.
properties.areaDesc: Eastern Las Animas County; Springfield Vicinity/Baca County

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 23 at 1:00PM MDT until July 23 at 3:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
South Central Rio Blanco County in northwestern Colorado...

* Until 345 PM MDT.

* At 100 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.3 and 0.6 inches of
rain have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts of 0.3 to 0.5 inches
are possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or
expected to begin shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow
moving through the Gorden, Shutte and Half Moon Gulches. The debris
flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of South Central Rio Blanco County

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 23 at 12:50PM MDT until July 23 at 2:45PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...West Central Jefferson County in central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 245 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 1250 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is
ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area.
Between 0.5 and 1 inch of rain has fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Bergen Creek, Troublesome Creek, Bear Creek, North Turkey Creek
and Cub Creek.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Evergreen, Kittredge, Wah Keeney Park and Genesee.
